items.title: Approve Pierce email to Tiffany Gill about double-billing
items.subtitle: PIERCE_2 · SOON

details.body:
FACTS (verified Supabase + Stripe sync 2026-06-22)

Stripe customer cus_UWlrdrLcCjrxHG — one person, one payment method, two ACTIVE $29/mo founding subscriptions:

- Sub #1 (PRIMARY, older): sub_1TiHzrL920SKTEEiB9eKdyxq · tgill@phyllisbrowning.com · started 2026-05-16 · 3 deals in app · last sign-in 2026-06-02
- Sub #2 (DUPLICATE, newer): sub_1TXiJRL920SKTEEi9DzVpx0F · tiffanygillrealtor@gmail.com · started 2026-06-09 · ZERO deals · NEVER signed in

Both auto-renewing, neither cancelled. Strong signal this is an unintentional re-signup, not an intentional second login.

---

DRAFT EMAIL — for Heath approval before send

FROM: Pierce — Dossie Customer Success <heath@meetdossie.com>
TO: tgill@phyllisbrowning.com
CC: tiffanygillrealtor@gmail.com
SUBJECT: Quick question about your Dossie subscription

---

Hi Tiffany,

Pierce here from the Dossie team — I help Heath keep an eye on our founding members. Hope your June is treating you well.

A small thing popped up that I wanted to bring to you directly: it looks like there are two active Dossie subscriptions tied to your card — one under tgill@phyllisbrowning.com (your original founding signup back in May) and a second under tiffanygillrealtor@gmail.com that started June 9th. Both at the $29 founding rate, both currently auto-renewing.

I wanted to check with you before doing anything, because a few of our agents intentionally keep separate logins for their brokerage email and their personal email. So my first question is just: was the second account something you set up on purpose, or was it an accidental re-signup?

If it was unintentional, here's what I'd like to do — with your okay:

1. Cancel the gmail subscription (the newer one), keep the tgill@phyllisbrowning account as your primary.
2. Refund the charges from the gmail account back to your card.

Either way, your founding $29/mo rate is locked in for life — that's not at risk no matter which account you keep. And if you actually do want two separate logins (some agents prefer it for client-facing vs. internal work), just say the word and I'll leave both running.

Just a reply to this email is plenty — whichever direction works for you.

Thanks for being one of the original founding members. Heath built Dossie around folks like you, and he sees everything that comes through here directly if you ever want to send feedback his way.

Warmly,

Pierce
Customer Success — Dossie
heath@meetdossie.com

---

WHAT HAPPENS AFTER YOU APPROVE

1. Pierce sends the email via Resend (from heath@meetdossie.com, Pierce in from-display, reply-to also heath@meetdossie.com so you see her reply).
2. When she replies, a follow-up agent action issues the Stripe refund (Pierce does not have Stripe write permissions — that is a separate Atlas/Jarvis ticket).
3. Refund amount = whatever has actually been charged on sub_1TXiJRL920SKTEEi9DzVpx0F (likely $29-58 depending on whether the 2026-06-16 renewal cycle hit). Verify in Stripe dashboard before processing.

NOTES

- /api/heath-actions-create endpoint is currently 404 on production (lives on staging branch, not merged). Pierce inserted this row directly via Supabase service-role. When you merge staging to main the API will catch up; for now the row is here and will surface in the HUD ACTIONS panel.
- Adheres to locked memory: feedback_customer_emails_minimize_problem + feedback_founder_outreach_warm_tone.
- This is the FIRST agent-direct customer email under the new "let Pierce talk directly" policy. Setting the precedent gently — Pierce introduces herself, frames Heath as the builder, replies flow to Heath.

FULL DRAFT FILE: C:\Users\Heath Shepard\Desktop\Shepard-Ventures\Marketing\drafts\tiffany-gill-double-billing-2026-06-22.md

IF YOU WANT TO SIGN THIS YOURSELF — swap closing to Heath and drop the opener intro line. Easy switch.

items.title: Social pipeline: Sage reviewer killing 53% of Sage own drafts — top fix is Sage, not infra
items.subtitle: RIDGE_3 · SOON

details.body:
ridge_3 30-day social pipeline diagnostic done. Posted 115 of ~525 expected (78% drop-off).

TOP 3 CAUSES + OWNERS:
1. Sage Haiku reviewer auto-rejects 154 of 286 generated posts (53%), 35 after 3 regen attempts. OWNER: sage. Loosen "fabricated specifics" to whitelist Heath pain (4:30am, $400/file, Italy). Demote Dossie Mention Rule from hard-block to warning.
2. max_per_day=1 cap vs generator creating 2-4/platform/day; 6 posts stuck approved RIGHT NOW (3 twitter + 2 fb + 1 li). OWNER: atlas. Raise caps to FB=2, Twitter=3, LinkedIn=2, IG=2.
3. YouTube placeholder zernio_account_id + cron-send-for-approval dark since 2026-06-15 + ELEVENLABS_API_KEY env missing. OWNER: atlas env audit.

CROSS-CONFIRM: ridge_1 findings (scheduled_for, render-videos, IG dark, Haiku reviewer) all reproduced and quantified.

REPORT: C:\\Users\\Heath Shepard\\Desktop\\Shepard-Ventures\\Engineering\\session-handoffs\\sage-social-pipeline-diagnostic-2026-06-23.md

NO MAIN PUSH. Staging only. Recommend spawn sage_4 (reviewer loosen) + atlas_8 (env + caps) — do not need carter unless Twitter cap-bypass needs deeper code audit.

Estimated lift if all 3 fixes ship: 115 -> ~280 posts/30d (2.5x).

Go to apollo.io/sign-up. Plan: Basic, $59/mo, 10k credits. Card on file. Account email: heath@meetdossie.com. Cohort 1 = SA metro TX agents, ~3-8k verified emails. Pierce has the filter spec ready at Shepard-Ventures/Marketing/drafts/2026-06-24-apollo-filter-spec.md

Go to instantly.ai. Plan: Growth, $37/mo, 1 inbox + 2k emails/mo. Card on file. Account email: heath@meetdossie.com. Full campaign config at Shepard-Ventures/Marketing/drafts/2026-06-24-instantly-campaign-config.md

items.title: atlas_4 unblock: install Android Studio OR approve GitHub Actions CI
items.subtitle: ATLAS_SWEEP_2026_06_25 · SOON

details.body:
Jarvis Native Android (Capacitor) APK build is 13/17 done. 4 items blocked on local toolchain.

Option A — Install Android Studio interactively (~30 min, recommended): https://developer.android.com/studio. Includes JDK + SDK + emulator. After install, ping Jarvis and atlas_5 will finish the build (~10 min).

Option B — Approve GitHub Actions CI build (~1 hr first time YAML setup, no local toolchain). Pros: works from any machine. Cons: slower iteration.

No cost either way. Once unblocked: APK in your inbox same evening + sideload guide already at Desktop/jarvis-android-install.md.

items.title: hadley_1: 11 TREC forms still need legal-judgment schemas
items.subtitle: ATLAS_SWEEP_2026_06_25 · WHENEVER

details.body:
Hadley_1 shipped 3/14 forms (20-18, 40-11, 39-10). 11 left: 38-7, 23-20, 15-7, 37-7, 49-1, 11-9, OP-H, 36-10, 34-7, 47-1, Buyer Rep Agreement.

These aren't mechanical PDF fills — each is a TREC legal-judgment schema (field semantics, validation rules, conditional logic). Sweeper kept them pending instead of guessing.

Gating rule from your locked memory: NO new TREC fills ship until DocuSeal e-sign works end-to-end across all 8 templates (feedback_dossie_sign_must_work_before_new_ships).

Recommended sequence: (1) Verify DocuSeal e-sign live for the 3 already-shipped forms. (2) Then approve hadley_2 spawn with full context bundle to ship the remaining 11 in batches of 3.

No action needed tonight. Reply "resume TREC kb" when ready.
